relatedness_reasoning: Both files represent a bidirectional transaction flow between investments and accounts departments: inbound carries debit/credit instructions from investments to accounts, outbound carries settlement confirmations back from accounts to investments. Field concepts (fund_id, fund_name, amount, currency, timestamps, approval tracking) directly correspond, and the message types form a logical pipeline step.

mappings.reasoning: Both uniquely identify a batch/settlement; settlement_id is the accounts system's reference for the same logical batch.
Effective_date specifies when the transaction should execute; settlement_date reflects when it was actually executed in accounts.
Inbound target_department (accounts) becomes the outbound from_department as accounts now sends the settlement back.
Inbound source_department (investments) becomes the outbound to_department as the settlement returns to investments.
Currency code is directly copied; both constrained to GBP enum.
Settlement total likely combines or derives from both debit and credit totals from the instruction batch.
Approval_status (approved) transitions to settlement_status (completed) as the instruction batch is executed and settled.
Fund identifier is directly copied from individual instruction to settlement transaction.
Fund name is directly copied from individual instruction to settlement transaction.
Instruction_type (debit/credit) maps to transaction_type (transfer_in), though the outbound enum is narrower and may represent only credit/inbound transfers.
Transaction amount is directly copied from instruction to settlement.
Transaction_id from inbound becomes the reference field linking back to the originating instruction.
Approval_timestamp marks when the batch was authorized; settlement timestamp reflects when the transfer was processed.
No validation or verification flag exists in inbound schema; likely added by accounts system during settlement processing.
System identifier is generated by accounts system itself, not sourced from inbound data.
Approver email address is directly copied to approved_by field.
Approval timestamp is directly copied from inbound batch approval time.
Instruction description (business narrative) may be aggregated or summarized into settlement notes field.
